76345.subject: Meet The Tech Exec Using AI To Fix Gen Z’s Jobs Crisis
76345.body:
Forbes Edge

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/meet-tech-exec-using-ai-fix-gen-zs-jobs-crisis-forbes-magazine-qkbhe?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BU5RrJcjSSA%2BgqRQt4sKBeQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1DzxfWUXpuUsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrw5kkiz~8k-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrw5kkiz-8k
  
We’ve all seen the headlines about artificial intelligence threatening entry-level jobs and the impact it’s having on recent graduates. So this week, I spoke with tech executive Clara Shih, who is using her experience as a leader in the AI space to help young people build careers using the technology, rather than risk being replaced by it.  Below is a snippet of my profile on Shih, a former Forbes 30 under 30 honoree, who is on a mission to ensure that Gen Z isn’t left behind by the changes in the job market:  In August 2025, Clara Shih was working as the Head of Business AI at Meta when she and her team built an artificial intelligence agent for businesses that answered customers’ questions, remembered their preferences, and even offered product discounts to seal a sale. The tool, Shih says, was rapidly adopted by business owners who found it helped keep consumers engaged and streamlined their sales process.  “It was everything you would want when you’re a builder in technology,” recalls Shih, now 44, who was honored on the Forbes 30 Under 30 list in 2012 as cofounder and CEO of the client engagement platform Hearsay Systems. She became CEO of Salesforce AI in 2023, before moving to Meta the next year. (Hearsay was acquired by the digital platform Yext in 2024.) But the feedback from entrepreneurs using Meta’s new tool also opened Shih’s eyes to the real—and potentially negative—impact AI could have. “The feedback we got from the businesses using our software was that they no longer needed their staff,” she says. “We didn’t know whether we should cry or celebrate because we’ve been wanting it for so long and yet when we achieved our goal, we started to realize what the implications were.” At the time, Shih was also hearing stories from younger family members and recent graduates about how they were struggling to find work and how some even had their job offers rescinded. “I started looking into this problem more and what I found alarmed me,” she says. “We are in a youth unemployment crisis in this country.” According to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, 5.6% of recent graduates aged 22 to 27 are unemployed and 41.5% are underemployed. “These are smart, talented, ambitious young people who should be gainfully employed, but because of what’s been happening with AI automating entry level tasks, they’re being underutilized and it not only hurts

76343.subject: Zcash’s Big Rebuild
76343.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

———————————————————————————

Hi all, happy Wednesday! Crypto markets were mixed overnight as strength rotated into the Ethereum ecosystem and lending names, while AI and perps continued to cool off.

Today, we examine whether Zcash is solving the two biggest obstacles facing private cryptocurrencies: proving monetary soundness and scaling private transactions without sacrificing security.

Let’s get into it.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

The same AI pullback that has been prevalent in global equities is also happening onchain. The AI sector was the worst-performing sector over the last 24 hours (-4.9%) as concerns surrounding the moat and margins of frontier models continue to rise. 

However, most of the decline came from DEXE, which declined over 82%. For context, DEXE recently experienced a massive run-up, hitting an all-time high of $48.89 on July 13. This rally was primarily fueled by an exchange listing on July 9 and a subsequent short squeeze, rather than long-term fundamentals. When the buying momentum dried up in a thin market, the price was left highly vulnerable to a sharp reversal. 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ac6b42ab-9a67-408e-ac57-fecb29b59b21/image.png?t=1784725394)
Caption: 

Perps also disappointed (-2.7%), with HYPE leading the downward charge, falling -6.4%. Given that these have been two of the best-performing sectors YTD, pullbacks like these aren’t unexpected. 

Adding to HYPE's specific weakness is the recent onchain activity from Multicoin Capital. Data reveals that the venture firm initiated a massive [unstaking](https://x.com/voidHL/status/2079639289772507425?s=20) of 1.96 million HYPE tokens valued at approximately $120 million (spread across multiple wallets). With portions of their holdings already being [deposited](https://x.com/lookonchain/status/2079760524695454188?s=20) into exchanges like Coinbase Prime for apparent profit-taking, and Hyperliquid's standard 7-day unstaking queue delaying immediate liquidity, the market is likely pricing in the anticipation of this impending sell pressure.

The best-performing sectors were the Ethereum Ecosystem and Lending, as both printed 6% gains in the last 24 hours. 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/dbf6c2c2-582e-494a-a108-c0fe9dc27d3f/image.png?t=1784725394)
Caption: 

Within the Ethereum Ecosystem index,
